title: Industrial Design Services

alternatives.name: Product industrial-design and development consultancy
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the modeled $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band; verified normalized EBITDA controls when target financials are available.
Earns its largest pre-technology revenue line from creating product designs and specifications that optimize use, functionality, value, manufacturability, and appearance.

May provide design research, human factors, user interface or interaction work, materials and form development, mechanical or electronic development, visualization, prototyping, and production support when industrial design remains the primary business activity.
Serves product companies across consumer, medical, industrial, transportation, furniture, packaging, computer, office-equipment, or other physical-product categories.
Operates as an independently acquirable client-services firm rather than a captive manufacturer design department.
exclusion_criteria: Engineering-primary firms applying physical laws and engineering principles as their principal service, which Census classifies in 541330.

Model and prototype design-and-fabrication providers, advisory-only industrial-design consultancies, and related-service firms when another enumerated archetype supplies the largest recognized revenue line.

Manufacturers, product brands, architectural and interior designers, graphic designers, fashion and jewelry designers, and software or computer-systems design firms whose primary activity falls outside 541420.
Solo studios below the normalized EBITDA band and enterprise providers above it.
alternatives.rationale: The Census scope centers on product designs and specifications, and the NAPCS hierarchy devotes its broadest branch to industrial design services for products across furniture, transportation, consumer goods, computers, packaging, industrial machinery, and other products. First-party PwC/Surfaceink, Infosys/Kaleidoscope, and Kearney/TEAMS acquisition announcements independently describe acquired product-design and development consultancies serving physical-product clients. Applying that taxonomy and repeated operating form to the 131-firm modeled band supports a 79-firm base as the largest class-neutral archetype. The range is wide because neither SUSB nor the public NAPCS taxonomy cross-tabs target-band firms by primary product.

alternatives.name: Model and prototype design-and-fabrication provider
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the normalized EBITDA band and earns its largest recognized revenue line from designing and fabricating full-scale or reduced-scale physical models, mockups, or prototypes of product concepts.
Controls the client contract for model or prototype delivery and may perform industrial design as a secondary service.
Is primarily a design-service establishment rather than a production manufacturer or engineering-primary contractor.
exclusion_criteria: Product industrial-design consultancies, advisory-only consultancies, and related-service providers assigned to another enumerated archetype.
Contract manufacturers, tool-and-die shops, additive-manufacturing bureaus, testing laboratories, and engineering firms when those activities are primary.
Businesses outside the verified normalized EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: NAPCS separately identifies model design and fabrication as an in-scope 541420 product rather than a subtype of product design. The 24-firm base preserves this distinct materials-, vendor-, and equipment-using business model. No official target-band incidence is published, so the count remains an explicit estimate with a broad range.

alternatives.name: Industrial-design advisory and strategy consultancy
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the normalized EBITDA band and earns its largest recognized revenue line from advice and guidance on industrial design, product strategy, design research, portfolio direction, or related design decisions.
May deliver research, workshops, concepts, or design governance but does not earn its largest revenue line from completed product specifications or physical model fabrication.
Remains primarily an industrial-design consultancy rather than a general management, marketing, engineering, or software consultancy.
exclusion_criteria: Product design-and-development consultancies and model or prototype providers assigned to another enumerated archetype.
General management consulting, marketing research, engineering consulting, and brand-strategy firms whose principal activity is outside 541420.
Businesses outside the normalized EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: The official product taxonomy identifies industrial-design consulting as a distinct product. The 18-firm base reflects a smaller advisory-led population than firms producing designs and specifications, without treating consulting work embedded in a product-design engagement as a separate firm.

alternatives.name: Related drafting, research, training, or other design-services provider
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the normalized EBITDA band, remains properly classified in 541420, and earns its largest recognized revenue line from an in-scope related product such as drafting, industrial-design training, marketing research attached to industrial design, or another related design service.
Does not fit the product-design, model-fabrication, or advisory-led archetypes based on its largest revenue line.
exclusion_criteria: Drafting, training, market-research, or other firms whose primary activity is classified in another NAICS industry.
Firms already assigned to another enumerated archetype and businesses outside the normalized EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: NAPCS lists drafting, industrial-design training, marketing research, and other related products that 541420 establishments may provide. Only firms for which such work is the largest recognized revenue line enter this record, preventing secondary products from becoming duplicate firm counts.

residual.name: Other, mixed, or unclassified target-band industrial-design firms
residual.rationale: Residual equals the 131-firm modeled band less the 127 base firms allocated to the four mutually exclusive largest-revenue archetypes. It captures balanced mixed-service firms, classification error, and uncertainty from using an official product taxonomy and acquisition examples without a target-band product cross-tab.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition for 541420 Industrial Design Services
2022 Statistics of U.S. Businesses annual tables
NAPCS Product List for NAICS 54142 Industrial Design Services
PwC US agreement to acquire Surfaceink
Infosys agreement to acquire Kaleidoscope Innovation

target_archetype.selection_basis: The v4.2 dataset estimates 131 firms in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band, derived from the 20-to-99, 100-to-499, and 500-plus employee classes under a common margin assumption. SUSB does not identify the primary product of those firms. Census defines the industry around product designs and specifications, and NAPCS separates product industrial design, model design and fabrication, industrial-design consulting, and related products. The enumeration assigns each firm once by its largest pre-technology recognized revenue line. The product-design branch is the broadest official service family and matches the operating form described in multiple first-party acquisition announcements, so its 79-firm base exceeds the 24-firm model-fabrication and 18-firm advisory alternatives. Selection uses scope, product structure, and transaction form only. The leading count range overlaps the next-largest range, so selection uncertainty is true.

value_basis.scope: Source-free class-neutral employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost role structure for the selected U.S. target-band product industrial-design consultancy classified in NAICS 541420.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: Begin with recognized client revenue for design research, product strategy, concepts, specifications, visualization, design development, prototyping, and production support. Deduct only a documented outside-provider amount embedded in recognized revenue when the provider, rather than the consultancy, controls delivery and performance responsibility for that scope. Prototype, model-making, engineering, testing, or specialist subcontract costs remain inside controllable value-added revenue when the consultancy directs the work, bears delivery responsibility or commercial risk, or earns a markup. Any amount deducted as pass-through is excluded symmetrically from employee cash cost, controllable-contractor cash cost, and the role-weight pool.
approved_pass_through_categories: Documented client-directed third-party prototype, model, material, or fabrication charges reimbursed at cost with no consultancy markup, delivery control, inventory risk, or performance responsibility
Documented external laboratory, testing, certification, or regulatory charges reimbursed at cost when the outside provider controls the tested or certified scope

Documented specialist design, engineering, research, or other subcontracted services reimbursed at cost when the outside provider, rather than the consultancy, controls delivery and performance responsibility for that scope

roles.title: Design research, human factors, and product strategy
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for user and market research, ergonomic and human-factors inquiry, opportunity framing, product definition, and design strategy; excludes concept execution and client delivery management assigned elsewhere.

roles.title: Industrial and product design
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for concept generation, form and function development, sketching, visualization, CAD surfacing, materials and finish definition, and product-design specifications.

roles.title: Design engineering and production development
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for operator-controlled mechanical, electrical, manufacturing, documentation, and production-support work that translates product concepts into feasible deliverables.

roles.title: Prototype, model, and design validation delivery
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for employees and controlled contractors planning, fabricating, coordinating, testing, and refining prototypes and models; documented noncontrolled outside-provider pass-through is excluded.

roles.title: Program, project, and client management
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for scope definition, scheduling, budgets, cross-functional coordination, design reviews, documentation control, and day-to-day client stewardship.

roles.title: Business development, proposals, and marketing
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for new-business development, proposals, partnerships, account cultivation, portfolio communication, and demand generation; excludes billable design strategy.

roles.title: Executive and corporate operations
roles.rationale: Source-free class-neutral allocation for firm leadership, finance, contracting, people operations, internal technology, facilities, and general administration not assigned to a delivery role.

value_basis.rationale: No contemporaneous target-band source jointly measures the selected product industrial-design consultancy's employee cash cost, controllable-contractor cash cost, and mutually exclusive functional weights. The dataset's labor-share estimate combines unmatched vintages, and its occupation mix is measured at broad NAICS 5414 and led by interior and graphic designers, so neither is used as target-specific evidence. The seven product-design functions are therefore a source-free class-neutral assumption allocation: every role is ESTIMATE/NONE with no source claim, and the weights sum to 1.00. Distinct identifiers separate employee cash cost from operator-controlled contractor cash cost, while documented noncontrolled prototype and subcontract pass-through remains outside both pools.
caveats: The role weights are source-free structural assumptions, not observed staffing, payroll, or contractor-cost shares.
The weights allocate the combined employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost pool and do not establish either pool's dollar amount or share of revenue.
The identifiers distinguish employee cash cost from controllable-contractor cash cost but do not supply either amount.

Contractor cash cost belongs in the role pool only when the consultancy controls the delivered value added; a documented outside-provider pass-through is excluded from both controllable value-added revenue and the cost pools.

An approved prototype or subcontract category is not automatically deductible: every deducted amount requires target-specific documentation of accounting presentation, outside cost, lack of markup, and outside-provider delivery responsibility.
Do not substitute or relabel the broad NAICS 5414 interior- and graphic-design occupation mix as product industrial-design evidence.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau 2022 NAICS definition for 541420: https://www.census.gov/naics/?details=54&input=54&year=2022
source_hints.why: Defines industrial design around product use, functionality, value, appearance, materials, construction, mechanisms, and production considerations, while excluding engineering-primary work.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau 2022 Statistics of U.S. Businesses: https://www.census.gov/data/tables/2022/econ/susb/2022-susb-annual.html
source_hints.why: Provides firms, establishments, employment, payroll, and receipts by enterprise size and supports reconciliation of the 131-firm modeled band.

source_hints.source: Census NAPCS Product List for NAICS 54142 and 2022 Economic Census EC2200NAPCSINDPRD industry-by-products table
source_hints.why: Separates product industrial design, model design and fabrication, industrial-design consulting, and related products; current revenue and establishment incidence should be extracted if publishable for 541420.

source_hints.source: PwC/Surfaceink, Infosys/Kaleidoscope Innovation, Kearney/TEAMS, Capgemini/D+I, and comparable first-party acquisition announcements
source_hints.why: Identifies services, end markets, integration with engineering or consulting buyers, and recurring acquisition forms without supplying undisclosed transaction economics.

source_hints.source: Census microdata or a reproducible U.S. firm census jointly classifying target-band 541420 firms by largest recognized revenue product, end market, employee class, ownership, and service breadth
source_hints.why: Needed to replace the estimated 79, 24, 18, and 6 archetype counts with disjoint target-band observations.

source_hints.source: Target-firm contracts, invoices, audited or reviewed financial statements, general ledgers, job-cost detail, and quality-of-earnings reports separating service revenue, prototype materials, outside fabrication, testing, travel, freight, and other reimbursables
source_hints.why: Needed to establish recognized revenue, each permitted pass-through category, and a positive bounded controllable value-added denominator without double counting contractor costs.

source_hints.source: Target-firm payroll registers, contractor ledgers, time records, project accounting, functional headcount, and quality-of-earnings reports for product industrial-design consultancies in the modeled band
source_hints.why: Needed to establish employee and controllable contractor cash cost plus cost-weighted design, research, development, prototype, program-management, sales, and administrative roles.

source_hints.source: Completed target-band industrial-design transactions, confidential sale-process logs, buyer lists, bids, quality-of-earnings bridges, and purchase agreements with consistently defined enterprise value and normalized EBITDA
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish sale availability, access and win probability, and paid valuation while avoiding inference from announced but undisclosed transactions.

controllable_value_added: What recognized revenue categories appear in target contracts and financial statements, and are revenue recognition policies consistent across fixed-fee, time-and-material, retainer, licensing, prototype, and reimbursable arrangements?

Which prototype materials, outside fabrication, laboratory, certification, travel, freight, printing, or other charges are contractually and economically owed to third parties rather than controlled value added?

For each proposed pass-through, what outside invoice, client billing, markup, delivery control, warranty responsibility, and project risk establish whether it belongs outside controllable value-added revenue?

Are outside designers, engineers, researchers, model makers, and developers controlled delivery resources that remain in both the denominator and contractor cash-cost numerator rather than pass-through?
Can low, base, and high recognized revenue and approved pass-through produce a positive, bounded, internally consistent controllable value-added denominator?
operational_realization: Which concept generation, research synthesis, sketching, CAD surfacing, visualization, documentation, simulation, design-system, project-management, and client-collaboration workflows are used in governed production across a material share of projects?

What implementation time, data preparation, integration, training, review, security, intellectual-property control, rework, hardware, software, and recurring vendor cost are observed for matching firms?
What fractions of the full annual removable cash-cost opportunity are operationally realized in each of years one through five, before commercial retention?
What upfront and annual implementation or change investments are required, expressed on the same opportunity basis and without treating vendor availability or isolated use as firm-level realization?
commercial_retention: What shares of target revenue and gross profit come from fixed-fee milestones, time and materials, retainers, value-based fees, licensing, prototype deliverables, and reimbursables?

How do contract renewal, competitive rebidding, client procurement, utilization targets, promised turnaround, scope expansion, and change orders affect whether operating savings remain with the consultancy?
How frequently are prices reset, and do client agreements share hours saved, fix deliverable prices, cap fees, or permit the provider to retain execution efficiencies?
What year-by-year commercial-retention schedule is supported over five years, separate from implementation investment, employee removability, and constant-price service demand?
sale_availability: What share of the 131-firm modeled band matches each disjoint largest-revenue archetype after excluding captive design departments, subsidiaries, engineering-primary firms, manufacturers, and businesses outside normalized EBITDA bounds?
How many economically distinct selected-archetype firms are independent rather than brands, offices, or subsidiaries under common ownership?

What proportion becomes genuinely available for sale over five years based on completed sales, active processes, ownership-transition evidence, and direct seller data rather than generic demographic assumptions?
How do founder reputation, named-designer dependence, client concentration, intellectual-property restrictions, employee retention, and portfolio ownership affect sale availability?
buyer_access_win: For the frozen reference buyer, what conditional share of sale-available targets can be sourced, accessed, diligenced, and won at the prevailing market-clearing price?

How many distinct strategic consultancies, engineering groups, technology-services firms, and sponsor-backed platforms compete for selected-archetype targets, without counting multiple brands under common control separately?
Which seller preferences, geographic limits, client-conflict rules, talent-retention demands, process access, management rollover, and strategic-buyer synergies affect access and win probability?
Keep access and win probability separate from the paid purchase multiple and do not reward willingness to overpay.
valuation_robustness: What disclosed completed transactions apply to independent U.S. product industrial-design consultancies with $1 million to $10 million normalized EBITDA?

How should EBITDA normalize owner or principal compensation, utilization, contractor cost, prototype pass-through, software and hardware expense, research or engineering mix, and nonrecurring project revenue?
What downside exit multiple applies to the same explicitly frozen year-five operating state, size, service mix, accounting basis, customer concentration, and management depth?

Are enterprise value, assumed debt, normalized working capital, equipment, prototype facilities, intellectual property, earn-outs, rollover equity, seller financing, acquired cash, and integration obligations treated consistently?
Do not use announced acquisitions with undisclosed terms, public consulting-company multiples, revenue multiples, or owner-operator SDE transactions as direct target-band EBITDA evidence.
operator_survival: At constant real service prices and stated quality, what share of baseline controllable value-added demand still purchases accountable industrial-design services from the selected operator archetype in year five?

Which client needs still require contextual research, physical-product judgment, human factors, safety, materials, manufacturability, prototype learning, stakeholder alignment, intellectual-property stewardship, and accountable delivery?
Which demand can shift to manufacturer design teams, engineering firms, management consultancies, software vendors, freelancers, direct self-service, or integrated product-development suppliers?
How do end-market cyclicality, project concentration, client insourcing, key-person dependence, talent retention, and the boundary with engineering affect independent operating continuity?

Keep price and contractual pass-through in commercial retention, implementation cost in operational realization, pass-through outside the denominator, and valuation multiples outside the survival measure.

special_notes: This outcome-blind specification defines only industry scope, target enumeration, denominator policy, evidence routes, and research questions; it contains no downstream economic result or classification.

The selected archetype is the largest estimated band archetype rather than the most attractive one. Selection uses official scope, official product structure, and first-party transaction form before economic research.

The enumeration follows a one-record policy: assign each firm once by its largest pre-technology recognized revenue product. Do not count a consultancy's product design, prototyping, research, consulting, drafting, or training offerings as separate firms.

The 79-firm selected-archetype base is not an observed Census count. The leading and second-place ranges overlap, so selection uncertainty is explicit and later joint-coverage evidence must be allowed to change the allocation without changing the selection rule.

The 131-firm modeled band is rated LOW quality and includes the 20-to-99, 100-to-499, and 500-plus employee size classes through size-class averages and a broad-sector margin; it is not a direct count of firms with $1 million to $10 million EBITDA.

The dataset's 0.9177 labor-share estimate is not used as the controllable value basis. Its vintage mismatch, harmonization, and implausible relationship to receipts require target-level reconciliation.

The four-digit 5414 occupation mix is not a target-specific role basis: interior and graphic designers are sibling-industry roles, and industrial designers are not separately represented among the leading listed occupations.
Engineering-primary design belongs in 541330, while fashion, footwear, jewelry, and similar design belongs in 541490. Each candidate must be classified by principal activity before inclusion.

Third-party prototype, testing, and reimbursable costs may leave controllable value-added revenue only when invoices and contracts establish pass-through. Controlled freelance or subcontracted design and development cost remains inside the denominator and may enter the contractor cash-cost numerator if a valid basis is established.
First-party acquisition announcements support operating-model and buyer-type evidence only; undisclosed price, EBITDA, sale availability, and win probability remain unobserved.
